A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BETWEEN MOUNT UPTON AND GILBERTSVILLE NEAR ROUTE 51 AROUND 935 PM. THE TORNADO GREW TO AN EF1 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 110 MPH NEAR THE GILBERTSVILLE-MOUNT UPTON CENTRAL SCHOOL. AT THIS POINT THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AT ITS STRONGEST WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT PASSED SOUTH OF GILBERTSVILLE ENDING A COUPLE MILES EAST OF GILBERTSVILLE.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE TOWN OF CAMPBELL...JUST WEST OF HOUGH ROAD...AT ABOUT 804 PM. THE TORNADO REACHED A PEAK INTENSITY OF EF1...AT SEVERAL POINTS ALONG ITS PATH. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 100 MPH...WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF AROUND 150 YARDS. HEAVY TREE DAMAGE WAS SUSTAINED. TWO HOMES...A STORAGE BARN...AND A CAMPER WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE DAMAGE PATH ENDED PAST THE INTERSECTION OF DUG AND RUMSEY ROADS...JUST WEST OF THE STEUBEN SCHUYLER COUNTY LINE.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BETWEEN MOUNT UPTON AND GILBERTSVILLE NEAR ROUTE 51 AROUND 935 PM. THE TORNADO GREW TO AN EF1 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 110 MPH NEAR THE GILBERTSVILLE-MOUNT UPTON CENTRAL SCHOOL. AT THIS POINT THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AT ITS STRONGEST WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT PASSED SOUTH OF GILBERTSVILLE ENDING A COUPLE MILES EAST OF GILBERTSVILLE.

SEVERAL HOMES SEVERELY DAMAGED

EF2 TORNADO WITH MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 130 MPH OCCURRED IN ERIN NY. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.7 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 0.25 MILES. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED. A WELL-CONSTRUCTED BARN COLLAPSED WITH DEBRIS FROM THE BARN THROWN THROUGH THE SIDING OF A HOME ACROSS THE STREET. AN 11000 POUND CAMPER WAS FLIPPED OVER A 5 FOOT FENCE BEFORE COMING TO REST ON ITS SIDE. A MOBILE HOME AND GARAGE NEXT DOOR WAS COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED WITH HEAVY DEBRIS STREWN NEARLY ONE HALF MILE DOWNSTREAM.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS POSSIBLE TORNADO. 6 HOMES DAMAGED AND NOT HABITABLE...NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.
POSSIBLE TORNADO...4 TO 5 HOMES WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.73 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 0.15 MILES PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE. A METAL OUTBUILDING COLLAPSED AND ONE BARN RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE. ONE HOUSE SUSTAINED ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE. A SMALL BRANCH WAS IMPALED INTO A CAR WINDSHIELD. A HOME ANEMOMETER RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 90.3 MPH.

STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF NEARLY 100 MPH WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.70 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 0.45 MILES PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE. TREES FELL ON THE ROOF OF A HOUSE. FURTHER IN ITS PATH A HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE AS THE ROOF WAS PEELED UP AND STREWN 30 YARDS. MINOR SIDING DAMAGE OCCURRED TO OTHER HOUSES NEARBY.
TREES SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE ITHACA-DANBY TOWN LINE SOUTH OF ITHACA COLLEGE
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN
POSSIBLE TORNADO. SEVERAL TREES. ANSD POLES DOWN...ROOF OFF HOUSE.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS POSSIBLE TORNADO. 6 HOMES DAMAGED AND NOT HABITABLE...NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.

AN EF2 TORNADO WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 8 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 400 YARDS PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE AND SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. HUNDREDS OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED IN A DENSELY FORESTED AREA. A HOUSE TRAILER WAS LIFTED AND DEMOLISHED...AND A TWO STORY BARN WAS ALSO DESTROYED. A SMALL POND HAD ALL OF THE WATER SUCKED OUT OF IT...AND A JEEP WAS MOVED SEVERAL FEET AND HIT WITH DEBRIS. A FEW SMALL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN NORTH ROAD AND CENTER ROAD AREAS.

A SIGNIFICANT SWATH OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON WALDRON ROAD. THESE TREES WERE ALL POINTING TO THE SOUTHEAST IN A SLIGHTLY DIVERGENT PATTERN. THIS DAMAGE OCCURRED CONCURENTLY WITH A TORNADO TRACK JUST TO THE EAST.
ROUTE 14 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF APRIL 28 ABOUT 4.5 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHEAST OF MCDONOUGH AND MOVED NORTHEAST CUTTING A PATH THROUGH HEAVY WOODS. THE TORNADO PASSED JUST SOUTH OF LAKE LUDLOW WHERE A COUPLE HOMES WHERE DAMAGED BY THE STORM. THE TORNADO PATH APPEARED TO BE ITS STRONGEST AND WIDEST JUST SOUTH OF THE LAKE THROUGH THE WOODED AREA. IT CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST WHERE THE PATH NARROWED AGAIN AND WEAKENED. THE TORNADO LIFTED LESS THAN 2 MILES SOUTH OF EAST MCDONOUGH.
TREES DOWN. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE REPORTED.

AN EF1 TORNADO...WITH MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 95 MPH...TOUCHED DOWN IN THE TOWN OF COLUMBUS IN NORTHEASTERN CHENANGO COUNTY...AT ABOUT 430 AM. THE TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG...FOR A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES...AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS...BEFORE LIFTING AGAIN AT ABOUT 435 AM. MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED...ALONG WITH SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...ALONG AND JUST TO THE LEFT OF THE PATH. THE UPPER STORY OF A TWO STORY HORSE BARN WAS ALSO TORN OFF.
AT LEAST 60 LARGE TREES DOWN IN A SWATH.

STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF AROUND 95 MPH OCCURRED...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1 MILE...AND A PATH WIDTH OF AROUND 500 YARDS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED. THE DAMAGE WAS CONCENTRATED NEAR MARKET STREET...AND TO THE WEST OF HUNTSVILLE RESERVOIR. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED...WITH A FEW UPROOTED.
